content.text: RBAC is in place. Now I'll deploy the full cluster: headless service, public service, StatefulSet, and a PodDisruptionBudget.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --cache=.25 \
            --max-sql-memory=.25
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Apply CockroachDB services, PDB, and StatefulSet

content.thinking:
Only 1 PVC exists and only 1 replica is ready. The StatefulSet is not scaling up. This might be because:
1. crdb-cluster-0 is not yet Ready (it's Running but 0/1 READY), so the StatefulSet is waiting for it to be ready before creating the next pod
2. The readiness probe is failing because the cluster hasn't been initialized yet

The issue is that CockroachDB pods won't become ready until the cluster is initialized with `cockroach init`. The StatefulSet creates pods one at a time in order, and only proceeds to the next pod when the current one is ready.
I'm seeing a classic bootstrap problem here: the first pod can't pass its readiness checks until the cluster is initialized, but I can't initialize the cluster until all the pods are running. I could fix this by relaxing the readiness probe requirements, switching to parallel pod creation instead of sequential, or removing the readiness probe entirely.

The cleanest solution is to use `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all three pods spin up at the same time, then I can run the initialization command once they're all available.

content.text: The StatefulSet is stuck at 1 replica because `crdb-cluster-0` isn't becoming Ready before init is run. CockroachDB needs all nodes up before `cockroach init` — I need `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` to create all pods at once.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb describe pod crdb-cluster-0 2>&1 | grep -A5 "Readiness:"

input.description: Delete StatefulSet to recreate with Parallel pod management

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --cache=.25 \
            --max-sql-memory=.25
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF

for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null || true)
  RUNNING=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "Running" || true)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) pods:"
  echo "$STATUS"
  if [ "$RUNNING" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods Running!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
